source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Equilateral triangle $ABC$ has side length $2$. A semicircle is drawn with diameter $BC$ such that it lies outside the triangle, and minor arc $BC$ is drawn so that it is part of a circle centered at $A$. The area of the “lune” that is inside the semicircle but outside sector $ABC$ can be expressed in the form $\sqrt{p}-\frac{q\pi}{r}$, where $p, q$, and $ r$ are positive integers such that $q$ and $r$ are relatively prime. Compute $p + q + r$.\n<image1>

image_dependency: question_text is geometry-heavy and explicitly refers to spatial objects and regions: an equilateral triangle, a semicircle on diameter BC, a minor arc centered at A, a sector, and a lune
asset_registry_record.image_manifest shows an associated diagram image exists
multi_step: question_text requires combining multiple geometric constructions: triangle ABC, a semicircle with diameter BC, and an arc from a circle centered at A
the target is an area of a lune defined by set differences ('inside the semicircle but outside sector ABC'), which implies multi-part geometric reasoning
the problem also asks to rewrite the result in the form sqrt(p)-qpi/r and then compute p+q+r, adding an extraction step after the geometry derivation
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single number ('10')
question_text specifies a canonical algebraic form sqrt(p)-qpi/r with q and r relatively prime, which constrains the final result

risk_flags: requires precise geometric interpretation of regions

decision_reasons: 资产完整：题干、答案和配图均存在，且注册检查通过、无登记问题
虽为强依赖图形的几何题，但图像资产已提供，核心语义支撑充分
答案为明确数值且题目给出规范化表达形式，具备较强可验证性
